response.justification: The candidate is a seasoned full-stack/web engineer with nearly two decades of experience, but his profile does not match this role's core requirements. He lacks native-level Japanese (only English, Romanian, and basic German), has minimal C/UNIX systems experience (a brief ~7-month stint at National Instruments in 2014-2015), and shows no evidence of Linux kernel analysis or modification, L2/L3 networking, network security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X), or embedded firmware development for switch products. His strengths in TypeScript/Node.js web platforms are largely irrelevant to embedded LAN-switch security development.
strengths: Long, varied software career (19+ years) spanning web, mobile, desktop, and some embedded exposure
Strong software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, pairing) and open-source contributions
Comfortable with Linux environments and low-latency systems work (DLang at Dunnhumby)
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency — a hard requirement for this role
Very limited professional C experience and no demonstrated Linux kernel investigation or modification
No background in network security (MACsec, 802.1X), L2/L3 switching, or embedded networking firmware
